strategies.name: Swapped select with constant on op0
strategies.target: Mutate binop-select tests to `op C, (select ...)` for add/and/or/xor/mul/fadd/fmul.
strategies.rationale: Patch adds commutative retry on operand 1 and recomputes const-ness from operand 0. This is the exact new behavior to stress.
strategies.expected_issue: Missed fold or wrong fold if swapped-path computes SimplifyBothArms incorrectly.

strategies.name: Swapped select with flags
strategies.target: Mutate existing flagged tests with select as operand 1 and constants/NaN/Inf on operand 0.
strategies.rationale: FoldOpIntoSelect may clone an arm; new path exposes more cases for `nsw/nuw` and FP fast-math folds.
strategies.expected_issue: Incorrect poison/FM semantics if flags are mishandled on cloned unsimplified arm in new operand order.

strategies.name: Swapped select multi-use guard
strategies.target: Use existing `*_use` patterns; swap operands so select is op1 of commutative op and keep extra select use.
strategies.rationale: FoldOpIntoSelect requires one-use select unless explicitly allowed. Ensure new path does not bypass this.
strategies.expected_issue: Illegal transform of multi-use select in swapped path.

strategies.name: Swapped PHI safety
strategies.target: Mutate PHI-based tests so commutative binop uses PHI as op1; include critical-edge/backedge/unreachable predecessor variants.
strategies.rationale: Patch now calls foldOpIntoPhi on operand 1 for commutative ops. Must preserve existing CFG safety constraints.
strategies.expected_issue: Unsafe PHI fold/speculation when PHI is operand 1 (critical edge/backedge/unreachable).

strategies.name: Bool/vector poison corner cases with swapped select
strategies.target: Mutate logical/vector select tests so select is op1 for commutative binops, with undef/poison in conditions/lanes.
strategies.rationale: FoldOpIntoSelect has early exits and poison-sensitive behavior; new operand order should be equally constrained.
strategies.expected_issue: Incorrect handling of i1/vector-i1 select or poison/undef lane corner cases in swapped path.

reason_thou:
1. Fix Understanding
The patch changes `foldBinOpIntoSelectOrPhi` from only checking operand 0 to also checking operand 1 when the binop is commutative. It introduces a helper lambda `TryFoldOperand(OpIdx, IsOtherParamConst)`. Semantically, this enables folds for commuted forms like `add C, (select ...)` and `and X, (phi ...)`.

2. Formal Semantic Modeling
Before: only transform if operand 0 is `select`/`phi`.
After: same, plus for commutative ops also try operand 1 with corresponding const-ness of the opposite operand.
Potential behavioral difference is optimization reachability (new folds), not intended semantic change.
Example: `%r = add i32 5, (select i1 %c, i32 10, i32 %x)`.
- Old: usually unchanged by this helper.
- New: may become `select %c, 15, (add 5, %x)`.
For `%c=true,%x=7`: 15 both sides. `%c=false`: 12 both sides.

3. Assumptions Identified
- Commutativity is sufficient to swap which operand is folded.
- `IsOtherParamConst` computed with opposite operand remains correct under swap.
- Existing legality inside `FoldOpIntoSelect` / `foldOpIntoPhi` remains valid when invoked for operand 1.
- One-use and speculation guards are still enforced identically.

4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
- Cases where only one select arm simplifies and const-ness drives `SimplifyBothArms`; swapped path might diverge.
- Flagged ops (`nsw/nuw`, FP FMF) where cloning unsimplified arm could preserve problematic flags.
- Multi-use select where fold must be rejected.
- PHI incoming blocks that are critical/back edges or unreachable; speculation must not be introduced.
- i1/vector-i1 and poison/undef lane cases that should block or constrain folds.

5. Test Strategies
Use operand-order mutations of existing select/phi tests to force the new commutative-op1 path, then verify: fold happens when legal, does not happen when illegal, and flags/poison-sensitive behavior remain correct.

{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "original_ir": "
define i32 @test(i1 %c, i32 %a, i32 %b, i1 %d, <2 x i1> %m) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %c, i32 %b, i32 0
  %or1 = or i32 5, %sel
  %mu = add i32 %sel, 1
  %flgsel = select i1 %c, i32 1, i32 %b
  %flg = add nsw i32 10, %flgsel
  br i1 %d, label %t, label %f

t:
  br label %merge

f:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %p = phi i32 [ 7, %t ], [ 9, %f ]
  %addphi = add i32 3, %p
  %vsel = select <2 x i1> %m, <2 x i32> <i32 1, i32 2>, <2 x i32> <i32 0, i32 3>
  %vand = and <2 x i32> <i32 5, i32 6>, %vsel
  %e0 = extractelement <2 x i32> %vand, i64 0
  %e1 = extractelement <2 x i32> %vand, i64 1
  %s0 = add i32 %or1, %mu
  %s1 = add i32 %s0, %flg
  %s2 = add i32 %s1, %addphi
  %s3 = add i32 %s2, %e0
  %s4 = add i32 %s3, %e1
  %r = add i32 %s4, %a
  ret i32 %r
}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @test(i1 true, i32 4, i32 7, i1 false, <2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 false>)
  ret i32 %r
}
", "transformed_ir": "

define i32 @test(i1 %c, i32 %a, i32 %b, i1 %d, <2 x i1> %m) {
entry:
  br i1 %d, label %t, label %f

t:                                                ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

f:                                                ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f, %t
  %p = phi i32 [ 10, %t ], [ 12, %f ]
  %0 = add nsw i32 %b, 10
  %flg = select i1 %c, i32 11, i32 %0
  %sel = select i1 %c, i32 %b, i32 0
  %mu = add i32 %sel, 1
  %or1 = or i32 %sel, 5
  %vand = select <2 x i1> %m, <2 x i32> <i32 1, i32 2>, <2 x i32> <i32 0, i32 2>
  %e0 = extractelement <2 x i32> %vand, i64 0
  %e1 = extractelement <2 x i32> %vand, i64 1
  %s0 = add i32 %or1, %mu
  %s1 = add i32 %s0, %flg
  %s2 = add i32 %s1, %p
  %s3 = add i32 %s2, %e0
  %s4 = add i32 %s3, %e1
  %r = add i32 %s4, %a
  ret i32 %r
}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @test(i1 true, i32 4, i32 7, i1 false, <2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 false>)
  ret i32 %r
}

define i32 @test(i1 %c, i32 %x, <2 x i1> %m) {
entry:

  %orv = or i32 8, %sel
  %sel2 = select i1 %c, i32 1, i32 %x
  %addf = add nsw i32 10, %sel2
  br i1 %c, label %t, label %f

t:
  br label %merge
f:
  br label %merge
merge:
  %p = phi i32 [5, %t], [9, %f]
  %ap = add i32 3, %p
  %vsel = select <2 x i1> %m, <2 x i32> <i32 1, i32 2>, <2 x i32> <i32 0, i32 3>
  %vand = and <2 x i32> <i32 7, i32 7>, %vsel
  %e0 = extractelement <2 x i32> %vand, i64 0
  %e1 = extractelement <2 x i32> %vand, i64 1
  %u = add i32 %sel, 1
  %sum0 = add i32 %orv, %u
  %sum1 = add i32 %sum0, %addf
  %sum2 = add i32 %sum1, %ap
  %sum3 = add i32 %sum2, %e0
  %sum4 = add i32 %sum3, %e1
  ret i32 %sum4
}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @test(i1 true, i32 7, <2 x i1> <i1 true, i1 false>)
  ret i32 %r
}
